text.title: Cloud Run Traffic Management Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the cloud-run-traffic-management methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale + concrete remediation hook.

rule.statement: Canary rollouts MUST start at ≤5% traffic to the new revision and increase only after smoke tests + monitoring pass.
rule.rationale: Lower initial split caps blast radius from a bad revision while still producing real production telemetry.

rule.statement: Each new revision MUST be deployed with `--no-traffic --tag <name>` first, smoke-tested via the tagged URL, then promoted.
rule.rationale: Tagged URL allows production-equivalent smoke testing without exposing real users to the unreleased revision.

rule.statement: A/B experiments MUST pin two stable revisions and split traffic by percentage — never split between an active deploy and a tag.
rule.rationale: Splitting against an actively-changing target produces non-stationary experiments and noisy results.

rule.statement: Rollback MUST be performed by promoting a known-good revision to 100% traffic, not by redeploying a previous container image.
rule.rationale: Revision-pin rollback is atomic and instant; image-redeploy rollback can introduce regressions in unchanged config.

rule.statement: Stale revision tags MUST be cleaned up after promotion; expired tags pollute the Cloud Console and confuse on-call.
rule.rationale: Long-lived tags accumulate and obscure the active rollout state during incidents.

rule.statement: If the input fails ALL "Applies If" preconditions or matches ANY "Skip If" disqualifier, do not apply this methodology — route the task to the adjacent sibling instead.
rule.rationale: Even flat checklists need an explicit skip leaf in the decision tree; this rule is the universal terminal.
